questions.title: Compute the Nth Fibonacci Number Iteratively
questions.scenario_framing: You are an explorer mapping out a mysterious staircase where each new step's height is the sum of the two previous steps. To reach the top floor marked N, you need to calculate the exact height of that Nth step without climbing the whole staircase.
questions.problem_statement: Write a function `fibonacci(n: int) -> int` that returns the nth Fibonacci number (0-indexed, so fibonacci(0) == 0, fibonacci(1) == 1). The function must handle any non‑negative integer n efficiently.
questions.starter_code:
def fibonacci(n: int) -> int:
    """Return the nth Fibonacci number using an iterative approach."""
    # TODO: implement the function
    pass

rubric.name: Correctness
rubric.description: Function returns the exact nth Fibonacci number for typical inputs (e.g., n = 0, 1, 5, 10).

rubric.name: Edge Cases
rubric.description: Correctly handles n = 0, n = 1, and large n without overflow or performance issues.

rubric.name: Efficiency
rubric.description: Uses an O(n) iterative solution instead of exponential recursion or unnecessary nested loops.

rubric.name: Code Quality
rubric.description: Clear variable names, proper comments, and no dead code.

hints: Think about how each step depends only on the two steps before it.
Maintain two variables for the previous two Fibonacci numbers and update them in a loop that runs from 2 up to n.
Start with `a, b = 0, 1`; then for each i in `range(2, n+1)`, set `a, b = b, a + b`. Return `b` when n > 0, otherwise return 0.
questions.analogy_explanation: Imagine building a staircase where the height of each new step is the sum of the heights of the two steps beneath it. By keeping track of just the last two step heights (like two bricks you carry), you can quickly determine the height of any higher step without reconstructing the whole staircase each time.
